finding: Importing a planning card whose frontmatter is already in-progress can create a target ledger task with status in_progress but no active claim owner. next --claim then refuses the task because it is already in_progress, tasks takeover refuses because there is no active claim, repair-claim reports no repairable drift, task-view loops back to tasks status, and the only reset path requires emergency approval.
expectedBehavior: The normal operator lane should either import planning in-progress as an adoptable open/claimable state, preserve or derive a claim owner from planning lock metadata, or offer a first-class adopt/rebind command for in_progress tasks with null claimState. The first-layer skill/playbook should also expose the correct surface so agents do not need to discover it by trial CLI help.
evidenceOrRepro: Resolved by ATM-GOV-0232 evidence: node --strip-types tests/cli/next-claim-orphaned-in-progress.test.ts creates an imported in_progress task with no active claim and verifies next route exposes it as claimable while prepareTaskForClaim normalizes it to ready and assigns the current actor.
followUp: No emergency reset follow-up remains for the ordinary orphaned in_progress/null-claim adoption path. Keep scope-amendment edge cases covered by dedicated scope diagnostics.
